symptoms.symptom_name: Chronic pruritic interdigital scaling that has become macerated and white, with diffuse fine moccasin-type scaling on the soles

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Interdigital tinea pedis with fissuring and maceration
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Chronic interdigital scaling with maceration plus diffuse moccasin plantar hyperkeratosis is the hallmark distribution of a dermatophyte foot infection.

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Candidal intertrigo of the web space
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Candida typically stays confined to the moist web space and does not produce diffuse dry moccasin scaling of the soles.

symptoms.symptom_name: Deep (~1 cm) longitudinal interdigital fissure, raw and erythematous at the base, weeping serous fluid with a foul malodour and severe tenderness

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Interdigital tinea pedis with fissuring and maceration
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Mechanical fissuring of waterlogged, macerated interdigital skin is a recognised complication of severe interdigital tinea pedis.
The open weeping fissure is the portal of entry that makes secondary bacterial cellulitis the important-not-to-miss complication to screen for and safety-net.

symptoms.symptom_name: Prolonged occlusive footwear and marked hyperhidrosis (heavy enclosed work boots, back-to-back long shifts, persistently damp feet)

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Interdigital tinea pedis with fissuring and maceration
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A warm, moist, occluded foot environment is the classic precipitant of dermatophyte overgrowth and interdigital maceration.

symptoms.symptom_name: Afebrile, no ascending hot spreading erythema or red streaks up the foot/leg, no purulent discharge, no regional lymphadenopathy

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of systemic and ascending spreading features makes an established secondary cellulitis unlikely now, though it remains a mandatory safety-net target given the open fissure.

symptoms.symptom_name: Severe mechanical pain on weight-bearing with temporary loss of mobility (hobbling on the heel, unable to wear a shoe)

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Interdigital tinea pedis with fissuring and maceration
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A deep painful fissure readily explains severe weight-bearing pain; drives symptom relief and off-loading advice but is non-specific for the organism.

symptoms.symptom_name: No toenail involvement, no dorsum-of-foot involvement, opposite foot unaffected

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Footwear contact allergy characteristically affects the dorsum of the foot; sparing of the dorsum with deep interdigital and plantar involvement argues against it and towards a dermatophyte.

symptoms.symptom_name: Clinician-measured vitals and weight (BP 128/82, HR 88, Temp 37.1, RR 16, SpO2 99%, Wt 85 kg) and palpation for ascending cellulitis/lymphadenopathy

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Sealed in-person measurement/examination; afebrile, haemodynamically stable and no palpable ascending cellulitis or lymphadenopathy argues against systemic infection. Not obtainable in telehealth without a device and hands-on examination.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Prompt in-person GP examination of the foot to exclude early secondary cellulitis and confirm the diagnosis
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: The one assessment telehealth cannot do — palpation for heat/induration/tenderness beyond the fissure and for inguinal lymphadenopathy, and direct inspection of the fissure depth and plantar surface.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Skin scraping for fungal microscopy and culture
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Confirms the dermatophyte versus candida and guides therapy if the response is poor; not required before starting empirical topical treatment.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Wound swab for bacterial microscopy and culture
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Not indicated now — no fever, ascending erythema, red streaks or frank purulence. Reserve for if secondary bacterial infection develops.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Systemic bloods / septic screen / blood cultures
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: No systemic features; ordering these now would be over-investigation of a localised skin condition.
